findings.description: Trigger-detection block now correctly iterates the parsed JSONL lines (lines 94-129): each line is `json.loads`-ed, `role == "assistant"` is required (line 106), the `content` field is normalized into a list of blocks (lines 109-122) handling list / string / JSON-encoded-string / non-list fallback, and the match predicate at lines 123-127 requires `block.get("name") == "Skill"` AND `(b.get("arguments") or {}).get("skill", "") == skill_name`. No regex / substring match against the raw text remains. Cosmetic-only change rejected? No — the implementation is structural: json.loads of each JSONL line, structured content-array walk, deterministic arguments.skill compare. The comment at lines 102-105 explicitly notes D22 and the prohibition on substring matching. The docstring at lines 33-36 states the contract. Carry-over from review #1 finding #1 is RESOLVED.

Explicit session_id error path is in place at lines 67-72: a `RuntimeError` is raised when `session_id` is missing from the parsed JSON output. The error message includes the subprocess `returncode`, a `stderr` excerpt (first 500 chars), a `stdout` excerpt (first 500 chars), and the hint to check `--output-format json` support. A complementary JSONDecodeError path is also present at lines 56-63 with the same diagnostic fields. Neither silent default (`session_id = ""` or `None`) nor a warning-only fallback remains. Carry-over from review #1 finding #4 is RESOLVED.

precommit_hooks_list: Per-file 500-line cap + plan footer drift + budget table Total + per-cell guard

verdict_rationale: All three D4.1 fixes are verified by direct file inspection and grep evidence. Fix #1 (structured JSON parse): run_eval.py lines 94-129 iterate json.loads-parsed JSONL lines, filter on role == 'assistant', normalize content into a list of blocks (list / string / JSON-encoded string / non-list fallback), and apply a deterministic `block.name == 'Skill' AND arguments.skill == skill_name` predicate — no regex/substring. Fix #2 (D23 extension): legacy `with_skill|without_skill|new_skill|old_skill` strings appear ONLY inside `skills/migration-claude-skill-creator/` (MIGRATION.md, MIGRATION.patch, research/*.json — all are documentation/reference data), and the new names `skill_active|baseline|skill_iter_n|skill_iter_prev` appear in all 5 expected production files (SKILL.md, scripts/aggregate_benchmark.py, references/schemas.md, agents/analyzer.md, eval-viewer/viewer.html). MIGRATION.md has 4 new rows with site_id pattern `D23-ext-N` (rows 24-27) and migrations_applied.json has 4 new entries (site_id `D23-ext-1` through `D23-ext-4`). Fix #4 (explicit session_id error): run_eval.py lines 67-72 raise RuntimeError on missing session_id with returncode + stderr excerpt + stdout excerpt + `--output-format json` hint; JSONDecodeError path at lines 56-63 carries the same diagnostic fields. Regression suite green: 619 pytest passed (1 unrelated warning), all 8 pre-commit hooks Passed (per-file 500-line cap, MIGRATION.md is generated, wemake-python-styleguide, flake8, ruff, ruff-format, black, mypy). All four WARNINGs from review #1 are RESOLVED. Carry-over INFO on residual 'Claude' prose mentions (review #1 finding #5) was not in the D4.1 fix scope and was noted as 'acceptable if D18 was sections-only' — out of scope for this gate. Likewise, NOTE on _subprocess.py Iterable vs list deviation (review #1 finding #7) is unchanged and remains acceptable per plan D16 line 193.
